calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-18-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Josie Corporon appeared by telephone for Edwin Van Eckhardt (Personal Representative).

Ms. Corporon explained that Laurence signed the waiver of accounting on behalf of Calvin because Debora was not available, that at the last hearing Debora was willing to waive an accounting and that she has been unable to obtain a signed waiver from Debora. In addition, Ms. Corporon stated that she explained to the parties that if Calvin received more than $5,000.00 it would require further administration, and this is why they arrived at the distribution amount of $5,000.00 to each of the Grandchildren. Furthermore, Ms. Corporon stated that the language in the order regarding distribution of after-discovered assets to Edwin and Elizabeth was an error on her part and that she is willing to strike that paragraph. Ms. Corporon is to provide a revised order reflecting that any after-discovered assets be distributed equally to the Children and the Grandchildren. The accounting petition is granted subject to receipt and review of the revised order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Continued To Jan-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-05-2016 Continued To Dec-19-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Continued To Dec-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Josie Porras Corporon for (petitioner) Edwin Van Eckhardt. Debora Van Eckhardt (mother of minor) also present.

The Court stated that there are a couple of issues. Ms. Corporon stated that they did not include a reference in the petition allowing the parent to receive distribution and waive the accounting citing 10954, that in regards to the additional writing they should have requested it be admitted as a codicil, that the family believed that decedent intended to leave it to her stepson and daughter in law. The Court directed counsel to 3(e)(2) and asked for clarification on the wording of "sell my worldly goods". Ms. Corporon clarified that it is "leave all my worldly goods", that what is being proposed is in line with what she wanted and that they wish to share. Ms. Corporon spoke to the Kuttler case stating that it is a reference that discusses implied intention. The Court asked for counsel's thoughts on a Guardian ad Litem. Ms. Corporon replied that Calvin's uncle is an attorney but did not think it necessary and that there is a provision for the parent to receive distribution. The Court stated that it is not sure that a GAL is necessary yet, that it will look at 10954, that counsel has until September 29, 2016 to file any additional argument or authority and that the accounting petition will be under submission as of 10/3/16.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)
